from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a city into a single fluent sentence.
    Each triple is turned into a short clause and the clauses are joined
    into one complex sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to turn a predicate into a readable phrase
    def phrase(triple: Triple) -> str:
        """
        Convert a triple into a natural‑language clause.
        Uses a mapping table for the most common predicates and falls back to a
        generic “subject predicate object” pattern.
        """
        import re

        # Normalise predicate (camelCase / snake_case -> lower case words)
        pred_raw = triple.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
        pred = re.sub(r'(?<!^)(?=[A-Z])', ' ', pred_raw).lower().strip()

        # Mapping from normalised predicate to a formatting lambda
        mapping = {
            "area of water":       lambda s, o: f"{s} has water covering {o}",
            "area of land":        lambda s, o: f"{s} has land covering {o}",
            "area total":          lambda s, o: f"{s} covers a total area of {o}",
            "population total":    lambda s, o: f"{s} has a population of {o}",
            "population density":  lambda s, o: f"{s} has a population density of {o}",
            "population metro":    lambda s, o: f"{s} has a metropolitan population of {o}",
            "elevation above the sea level": lambda s, o: f"{s} sits at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
            "utc offset":          lambda s, o: f"{s} observes a UTC offset of {o}",
            "time zone":           lambda s, o: f"{s} lies in the {o} time zone",
            "country":             lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
            "state":               lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the state of {o}",
            "capital":             lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
            "largest city":        lambda s, o: f"The largest city in {s} is {o}",
            "leader":              lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
            "leader title":        lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} holds the title of {o}",
            "county seat":         lambda s, o: f"The county seat of {s} is {o}",
            "headquarter":         lambda s, o: f"The headquarters of {s} are in {o}",
            "chairperson":         lambda s, o: f"The chairperson of {s} is {o}",
            "language":            lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
            "demonym":             lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
            "ethnic group":        lambda s, o: f"{s} is home to the {o} ethnic group",
            "postal code":         lambda s, o: f"The postal code(s) of {s} are {o}",
            "area code":           lambda s, o: f"The area code(s) of {s} are {o}",
            "is part of":          lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
            "type":                lambda s, o: f"{s} is a {o}",
            "government type":    lambda s, o: f"The government of {s} is {o}",
            "location":            lambda s, o: f"{s} is located at {o}",
        }

        formatter = mapping.get(pred)
        if formatter:
            return formatter(triple.subject, triple.object)
        # Generic fallback: keep predicate readable
        return f"{triple.subject} {pred} {triple.object}"

    clauses = [phrase(t) for t in triples]

    # Combine clauses into a single sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

  def phrase(triple: Triple) -> str:
          # Convert predicate from camelCase or snake_case to a readable phrase
          # e.g., "areaOfWater" -> "area of water", "population_total" -> "population total"
          import re
          pred_raw = triple.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
          # Insert space before capital letters (except at start) and lower case everything
          pred = re.sub(r'(?<!^)(?=[A-Z])', ' ', pred_raw).lower()
          # Simple patterns for a few common predicates
          if pred in {
              "area of water", "area of land", "area total", "population total",
              "population density", "population metro", "elevation above the sea level",
              "utc offset", "time zone"
          }:
              return f"{triple.subject} has a {pred} of {triple.object}"
          if pred == "country":
              return f"{triple.subject} is in {triple.object}"
          if pred == "state":
              return f"{triple.subject} is located in the state of {triple.object}"
          if pred == "capital":
              return f"The capital of {triple.subject} is {triple.object}"
          if pred == "largest city":
              return f"The largest city in {triple.subject} is {triple.object}"
          if pred == "leader":
              return f"The leader of {triple.subject} is {triple.object}"
          if pred == "leader title":
              return f"The leader title of {triple.subject} is {triple.object}"
          if pred == "county seat":
              return f"The county seat of {triple.subject} is {triple.object}"
          if pred == "headquarter":
              return f"The headquarters of {triple.subject} is {triple.object}"
